hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: Sprint 12 delivered a new security feature (admission webhook) with a new binary, new package, new Helm subchart, and threat model update. All gates passed first run. The 3-phase process handled this cross-cutting feature sprint (Go + Helm + Docker + docs) as effectively as pure Go sprints.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: All 4 core gates plus helm lint and smoke vet passed on first run. Coverage gate confirmed webhook package at 93.2%, validating test quality for the new security-critical package. Lint gate ensured no dead code or style regressions across 16 new files.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 5/5 H7 instructions from baseline were applicable and followed: (1) Defense in depth: webhook + audit logging + fail-closed + namespaceSelector scoping; (2) Tests assert behavior (allow/deny outcomes), not implementation; (3) TLS and config loading behind interfaces for testability; (4) Adversarial scenarios tested (spoofed labels, malformed body, missing body, subresource bypass); (5) Rejection messages include namespace, label, and allowed values.

hypotheses.name: Wave parallelism helps
hypotheses.evidence: Delegation ratio 17.7% with 6 subagents. Used for investigation (WebSearch for admission webhook patterns) and parallel independent file creation. Main implementation was sequential due to handler<->config dependency. Parallelism helped for research but not for core implementation.

hypotheses.name: Flowstate works for greenfield projects
hypotheses.evidence: Sprint 12 is the 13th sprint (0-12). Project now has 3 binaries, CRD support, 3 Helm subcharts, 82 unit tests + 13 smoke tests, and 93.2% webhook coverage. Process continues to scale as the project grows in complexity.
